summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer installs cross-platform crypto clipboard hijacker
details: The package name typosquats the popular @juggle/resize-observer ResizeObserver polyfill and ships that project's README verbatim, but the actual install behavior has nothing to do with a polyfill. The postinstall script (npm-install.cjs) escalates privileges via sudo/pkexec to install python3-pip, xclip, and wl-clipboard through the OS package manager; if pip is missing it fetches https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py to /tmp and runs it; it then `pip install --break-system-packages` a bundled Python package, clipboard-guardian. The Python daemon (guardian.py) monitors the user's clipboard and, when it detects a cryptocurrency address (BTC, ETH, SOL, XMR, TRX, and ~25 other chains), rewrites the clipboard contents to substitute the attacker's hardcoded wallet address before the user pastes — a classic clipper that redirects outgoing crypto transactions to the attacker. Hardcoded attacker wallets include ETH 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18, BTC bc1qs2mpls4..., XMR 42zhAidVhP7..., SOL DWBAmQLi9gP6mk7..., TRX TVgEgMemmJABYY..., and others, written into ~/.config/clipboard-guardian/config.json. Persistence is established under deceptive identifiers impersonating OS components: a Linux systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service`, a macOS LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist`, and a Windows HKCU Run entry `PyRuntimeBroker`; the Python process additionally calls setproctitle to masquerade as `python3-dbus-helper` / `com.apple.python.runtime`. Multiple independent block signals are present: typosquat name + payload, install-time privilege escalation with undocumented system mutation, persistent autostart under disguised service names, and credential-equivalent hardcoded attacker wallets distributed across 30+ chains.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
